basicDetails.schemeName: National Mission for Sustainable Agriculture - Reinforced Area Development

nodalDepartmentName.label: Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme "National Mission for Sustainable Agriculture - Reinforced Area Development" is a centrally sponsored scheme that aims to promote location-specific sustainable agriculture production by encouraging improved agronomic practices, soil health management, better rainwater utilization, judicious use of agrochemicals, crop diversification, and the progressive adoption of integrated farming systems involving crops, livestock, and trees.
schemeContent.benefits_md: - **Financial Help:** Provides financial assistance to eligible farmers to adopt sustainable agricultural practices. - **Improved Agronomic Practices:** Encourages the implementation of location-specific, modern, and efficient farming techniques. - **Soil Health Management:** Supports initiatives for maintaining and enhancing soil fertility and health. - **Enhanced Rainwater Use Efficiency:** Promotes techniques and infrastructure for optimal utilization of rainwater in agriculture. - **Judicious Use of Chemicals:** Advocates for reduced and responsible application of chemical fertilizers and pesticides. - **Crop Diversification:** Encourages farmers to cultivate a variety of crops, promoting resilience and better income opportunities. - **Integrated Farming Systems:** Supports the adoption of integrated crop-livestock-tree farming for holistic farm management.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme "National Mission for Sustainable Agriculture - Reinforced Area Development" aims to promote sustainable agriculture through improved agronomic practices, efficient resource use, and integrated farming systems by providing financial support to all farmers.

children.text: The scheme "National Mission for Sustainable Agriculture - Reinforced Area Development" is a centrally sponsored scheme that aims to promote location-specific sustainable agriculture production by encouraging improved agronomic practices, soil health management, better rainwater utilization, judicious use of agrochemicals, crop diversification, and the progressive adoption of integrated farming systems involving crops, livestock, and trees.
Provides financial assistance to eligible farmers to adopt sustainable agricultural practices.
Encourages the implementation of location-specific, modern, and efficient farming techniques.
Supports initiatives for maintaining and enhancing soil fertility and health.
Promotes techniques and infrastructure for optimal utilization of rainwater in agriculture.
Advocates for reduced and responsible application of chemical fertilizers and pesticides.
Encourages farmers to cultivate a variety of crops, promoting resilience and better income opportunities.
Supports the adoption of integrated crop-livestock-tree farming for holistic farm management.
The applicant may visit and apply online at the
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side, then click on "Beneficiary Registration" and select any one from the 'Farmer' or 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type.
Select the District, Taluka, and Village, choose 'what type of account holder you are?', enter your Account Number and Name, solve the Captcha, and click 'Save and Next'.
Enter your Mobile Number and Email address, click 'Send OTP', enter the received OTP, create and confirm a Password, solve the Captcha, and click 'Submit'.
The applicant may visit and apply online at the
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side of the homepage. On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Agricultural Schemes’.
Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button, and an application page will open.
Click on the "Apply New" button and submit a new application.
Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application.
Once the application is done, confirm it.
Take a printout of the confirmed application.

applicationProcess.process_md: > **Registration Process:** **Step 1:** The applicant may visit and apply online at the [I-Khedut](https://ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in/) Portal. **Step 2:** Click on &#39;Login&#39; from the top right-hand side, then click on &quot;Beneficiary Registration&quot; and select any one from the &#39;Farmer&#39; or &#39;Institutional&#39; or &#39;Except For the Farmer&#39; as the Beneficiary Type. **Step 3:** Select the District, Taluka, and Village, choose &#39;what type of account holder you are?&#39;, enter your Account Number and Name, solve the Captcha, and click &#39;Save and Next&#39;. **Step 4:** Enter your Mobile Number and Email address, click &#39;Send OTP&#39;, enter the received OTP, create and confirm a Password, solve the Captcha, and click &#39;Submit&#39;. > **Login Process:** **Step 1:** The applicant may visit and apply online at the [I-Khedut](https://ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in/site/) Portal. **Step 2:** Click on &#39;Login&#39; from the top right-hand side of the homepage. On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Agricultural Schemes’. **Step 3:** Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button, and an application page will open. **Step 4:** Click on the &quot;Apply New&quot; button and submit a new application. **Step 5:** Click on the &quot;Update Application&quot; button to add corrections to the application. **Step 6:** Once the application is done, confirm it. **Step 7:** Take a printout of the confirmed application. > **Contact Us:** [Click here](https://ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in/Public/frm_Public_Contacts.aspx).

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: - The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat. - The applicant should be a farmer.

children.text: The applicant should be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant should be a farmer.
